title: SpaceRipple: Lightweight Semantic Delivery for Mission-Oriented LEO Earth Observation Satellite Networks
abstract: arXiv:2606.26559v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Earth observation satellite networks generate massive volumes of high-resolution imagery, whereas inter-satellite and downlink resources remain limited. In many time-sensitive missions, ground users require mission-relevant semantic information rather than a full raw-image downlink. This paper proposes SpaceRipple, a lightweight framework for mission-oriented semantic delivery and on-board processing in Earth observation satellite networks. A sensing satellite performs adaptive compression and metadata generation to reduce inter-satellite traffic, while an edge computing satellite restores the received representation and extracts task-relevant semantic information. Unlike fidelity-driven image transmission, SpaceRipple coordinates compression, forwarding, restoration, and semantic inference within a collaborative pipeline, enabling semantic-oriented delivery instead of pixel-level image delivery. translated_title: SpaceRipple：面向任务的低轨地球观测卫星网络的轻量级语义传输
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26559v1 公告类型：交叉 摘要：地球观测卫星网络产生大量高分辨率图像，而星间和下行链路资源仍然有限。在许多时间敏感的任务中，地面用户需要的是任务相关的语义信息，而非完整的原始图像下行链路。本文提出了SpaceRipple，一个用于地球观测卫星网络中面向任务的语义传输与星上处理的轻量级框架。感知卫星执行自适应压缩和元数据生成，以减少星间流量；而边缘计算卫星则恢复接收到的表示，并提取任务相关的语义信息。与保真度驱动的图像传输不同，SpaceRipple在协作管道中协调压缩、转发、恢复和语义推理，实现语义导向的传输而非像素级图像传输。还引入了一种压缩感知的MoE增强模块。
tldr: 本文提出SpaceRipple，一种轻量级框架，通过星上自适应压缩和语义推理，实现面向任务的地球观测卫星网络中的语义级图像传输，取代传统像素级传输，显著降低星间和下行链路资源消耗。
en_tdlr: This paper proposes SpaceRipple, a lightweight framework that achieves mission-oriented semantic-level image delivery in Earth observation satellite networks through on-board adaptive compression and semantic inference, replacing traditional pixel-level transmission and significantly reducing inter-satellite and downlink resource consumption.
